core.name: CORE
core.fullDescription: Personal AI Infrastructure core. AUTO-LOADS at session start. USE WHEN any session begins OR user asks about identity, response format, contacts, stack preferences.

createskill.name: CreateSkill
createskill.fullDescription: Create and validate skills. USE WHEN create skill, new skill, skill structure, canonicalize. SkillSearch('createskill') for docs.

design.name: Design
design.fullDescription: Master design orchestrator for frontend work. USE WHEN working on CSS, components, UI, UX, styling, layout, responsive design, accessibility, a11y. Auto-triggers on frontend files. Finds or creates design specs. Invokes design:ux, design:a11y, design:responsive, design:trends sub-skills.

animationcopycat.name: AnimationCopycat
animationcopycat.fullDescription: Analyze video frames to reverse-engineer animations and produce Remotion recreation plans adapted to the Invela design system. USE WHEN recreating animations, analyzing video frames, copying animation styles, or planning Remotion compositions from reference material.

invela-brand.name: invela-brand
invela-brand.fullDescription: Use when working on Invela design system, UI components, or any visual design decisions. Provides verified brand colors, typography, iconography guidelines. Self-improving - updates with brand changes.

radix-primitives.name: radix-primitives
radix-primitives.fullDescription: Use when building UI components with Radix UI, styling Radix with Tailwind, or implementing accessible interactive patterns. Covers all 25+ primitives, data-state styling, and composition.

accessibility-wcag-22.name: accessibility-wcag-22
accessibility-wcag-22.fullDescription: Use when checking accessibility, validating contrast, focus states, touch targets, or any WCAG compliance. Context-aware - surfaces relevant criteria based on element type.

responsive-dark-mode.name: responsive-dark-mode
responsive-dark-mode.fullDescription: Use when designing for multiple screen sizes (320px-2560px+), implementing responsive layouts, or building dark mode. Covers breakpoints xs/sm/md/lg/xl/2xl and dark theme patterns.

financial-ux-patterns.name: financial-ux-patterns
financial-ux-patterns.fullDescription: Use when designing financial data displays, dashboards, risk visualization, data tables, or any fintech UX. Covers number formatting, compliance UI, and trust patterns.

design-systems-reference.name: design-systems-reference
design-systems-reference.fullDescription: Use when benchmarking design decisions against industry standards. References Material Design 3, IBM Carbon, Shopify Polaris, Adobe Spectrum, GitHub Primer, Salesforce Lightning, and more.

market-competitors.name: market-competitors
market-competitors.fullDescription: Use when researching competitor design patterns, differentiating Invela's UI, or benchmarking against market. Tracks 20+ GRC/TPRM/compliance competitors.

invela-design-system.name: invela-design-system
invela-design-system.fullDescription: Use when checking current DS state, making token decisions, or documenting component specs. Self-improving - updates as the design system evolves.

ds-audit-handoff.name: ds-audit-handoff
ds-audit-handoff.fullDescription: Use when tracking Radix-to-Invela deltas, creating handoff documentation for developers/AI agents, or auditing design system alignment.

vercel-web-interface.name: vercel-web-interface
vercel-web-interface.fullDescription: USE WHEN building any web UI, forms, interactions, animations, or performance optimization. Vercel's comprehensive interface guidelines covering hundreds of implementation decisions. Framework-agnostic with React/Next.js specifics.

figma-dev.name: figma-dev
figma-dev.fullDescription: Use when working with Figma Make, Figma MCP, or syncing design tokens between code and Figma. Ensures bidirectional compatibility.

commit.name: commit
commit.fullDescription: Smart commit with pre-commit checks and documentation refresh. Use when ready to commit changes to ensure quality and documentation stay in sync.

catchup.name: catchup
catchup.fullDescription: Restore context at session start. Use when starting a new session, returning to work after a break, or needing to understand current project state quickly.

knowledge-accumulation.name: knowledge-accumulation
knowledge-accumulation.fullDescription: Capture and reuse institutional knowledge. Use when solving tricky bugs, making architectural decisions, discovering integration quirks, or establishing reusable patterns.

fixing-test-suites.name: fixing-test-suites
fixing-test-suites.fullDescription: Use when fixing failing tests, especially auth mocking issues in Flask/pytest or React/Jest service mocking patterns.

test-patterns.name: test-patterns
test-patterns.fullDescription: Use when writing tests, organizing test suites, creating mocks/fixtures, or establishing testing conventions for a project.

api-testing.name: api-testing
api-testing.fullDescription: Use when testing REST APIs, GraphQL endpoints, writing integration tests for backends, or setting up API test automation.

react-performance.name: react-performance
react-performance.fullDescription: React and Next.js performance optimization. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring React/Next.js code, optimizing bundle size, or fixing performance issues.

ui-review.name: ui-review
ui-review.fullDescription: Audit UI code against 100+ best practices for accessibility, performance, and UX. Use when reviewing frontend code, checking accessibility, or auditing design implementation.

prd.name: prd
prd.fullDescription: Create product requirements documents. Use when starting a new feature, product, or project that needs formal requirements before implementation.

stories.name: stories
stories.fullDescription: Create user stories with INVEST criteria. Use when breaking down features into implementable units of work.

acceptance.name: acceptance
acceptance.fullDescription: Write acceptance criteria in Given-When-Then format. Use when defining done for stories, features, or bug fixes.

estimate.name: estimate
estimate.fullDescription: Estimate story points using Fibonacci scale with complexity analysis. Use when sizing stories for sprint planning.

prioritize.name: prioritize
prioritize.fullDescription: Prioritize work using RICE framework. Use when deciding what to build next or ordering a backlog.

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.fullDescription: Plan and track sprints. Use when starting sprint planning, reviewing sprint progress, or running sprint ceremonies.

status.name: status
status.fullDescription: Generate project status reports. Use when preparing daily standups, weekly summaries, or stakeholder updates.

raid.name: raid
raid.fullDescription: Maintain RAID logs (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies). Use when tracking project risks, validating assumptions, or managing dependencies.

decision-record.name: decision-record
decision-record.fullDescription: Create architectural decision records. Use when making significant technical or architectural decisions that should be documented for future reference.

docs-architecture.name: docs-architecture
docs-architecture.fullDescription: Use when structuring documentation for a project, product, or organization — choosing what to write, for whom, at what depth, identifying documentation gaps, or creating a documentation strategy from scratch.

diagram-standards.name: diagram-standards
diagram-standards.fullDescription: Use when creating SVG diagrams for documentation — architecture diagrams, flow charts, data flow diagrams, sequence diagrams, or dashboard wireframes — ensures accessible, brand-consistent, version-controllable diagrams.

AssetManagement.name: AssetManagement
AssetManagement.fullDescription: Brand asset management with constraint enforcement. USE WHEN creating images, logos, icons, colors, typography, visual assets, or auditing brand consistency for any project.
